description: In a busy coffee shop, Chandler and Joey sit on a couch facing a low table. Ross stands behind the couch, holding a multi-page letter in one hand. Looking at Chandler and Joey, Ross says, "To take responsibility for everything that went wrong in our relationship."
Ross gestures with the letter and leans forward slightly. He says, "I mean, she goes on for five pages about about how I was unfaithful to her." Joey looks up at Ross.
Ross lowers the letter slightly, maintaining eye contact with Chandler and Joey.
Ross leans further forward, his volume rising to a shout as he says, "We were on a break."
Chandler quickly brings both hands to his face, leaning his head back. He lowers his hands and says, "Oh, my God. If you say that one more time, I'm going to break up with you."
Ross looks away briefly, his jaw tightening.

Ross points a finger toward Chandler and says, "Fine. But this breakup was not all my fault." Ross then looks down at the letter in his hands and reads, "And she says here, if you accept full responsibility full"
Ross looks up at Chandler and Joey, raising his voice slightly on the word "responsibility?", then looks back at the letter, reading, "I can begin to trust you again."
Ross continues reading from the letter, "Does that seem like something you can do?"
Ross lowers the letter, looks directly at Joey, and asks, "Does it?"
Joey looks at Ross, his lips parted slightly. Joey then shakes his head slightly while maintaining eye contact. Chandler looks at Joey.
Joey says, "No."
Ross nods slightly, turning his gaze toward Chandler.

Chandler gestures with both hands open, his palms facing upward. He says, "Look, Ross, you have what you want. You're back with Rachel. If you bring this up now, you're going to wreck the best thing that"
Chandler finishes the phrase, "ever happened to". Ross quickly interrupts, saying, "I know. I know. You're right," while nodding his head and looking down.
Ross sits down in an armchair next to the couch, looking at the letter in his hands.
Ross sits in the armchair, waving the letter in his hand. He says, "Yeah, I guess I'll let it go. But you understand how hard it is to forget about this."
Joey gestures with his hands, his palms open, and says, "Sure, it's hard to forget."
Joey leans forward, resting his forearms on his knees, and says, "But that doesn't mean you have to talk about it."
Chandler looks at Joey. Joey stares straight ahead, his expression unchanging.
Joey maintains his gaze forward, his voice low, and says, "A lot of things happened on that trip that we should never, ever talk about."
Chandler and Ross look at Joey. Ross furrows his brow.
Ross leans forward in his chair, looking at Joey, and asks, "What the hell happened on that beach?"
Joey looks down at his hands, avoiding Ross's gaze.
Joey raises one hand slightly, looks at Ross, and says, "It's between us and the sea, Ross."
Ross looks at Joey, his eyebrows raised. Chandler continues to look at Joey.

The setting changes to an apartment hallway. Phoebe stands outside a closed door bearing the number 54. She knocks on the door three times, steps back, and grips her hands together. The door opens to reveal Ursula.
Phoebe says, "Hi, Ursula." Ursula, standing in the doorway, replies, "Hey."
Phoebe swallows, keeping her hands clasped in front of her.
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she speaks, "Okay, well, I know that we haven't talked in a long time but okay. Our mom is not our birth mom. This other"
Phoebe points toward Ursula and says, "lady is our birth mom." Ursula replies, "Right, okay. The one that lives in Montauk?"
Phoebe's eyes widen as she asks, "You know her?" Ursula shakes her head slightly and says, "No."
Ursula looks at Phoebe and says, "I read about her in Mom's suicide note."
Phoebe's jaw drops slightly, and she asks, "There was a suicide note?"
Ursula looks at Phoebe without changing her facial expression.
Phoebe leans forward slightly and asks, "Do you still have it?" Ursula replies, "Hang on," and turns away, walking into the apartment.
Phoebe stands alone in the hallway, looking through the open doorway. She gestures with one hand toward the doorframe.

Phoebe raises her voice, speaking into the apartment, "I can't believe you didn't tell me there was a suicide note." Ursula's voice is heard from off-screen inside the apartment, "Yeah, so how have you been doing?"
Phoebe closes her eyes briefly, exhales audibly, and drops her hands to her sides.
Phoebe looks toward the open doorway and says, "Shut up."
Phoebe puts her hands on her hips. Ursula returns to the doorway and hands Phoebe a folded piece of paper. Phoebe takes it and unfolds it.
Phoebe looks down at the paper and reads aloud, "Goodbye, Phoebe and Ursula. I'll miss you. P.S. your mom lives in Montauk."
Phoebe lowers the paper, looks at Ursula, and says, "You just wrote this."
Ursula shrugs slightly and says, "It's pretty much the gist."
Phoebe stares at Ursula, her lips parted slightly.
Ursula looks at Phoebe and says, "Except for the poem. You read the poem, right?"
Phoebe shakes her head and says, "No."
Ursula raises her eyebrows slightly.
Ursula says, "All right, hang on," and turns to walk back inside.

Phoebe stands in the hallway, looking at the piece of paper in her hand. The setting changes to the interior of an apartment. Chandler, Monica, and Joey sit around a circular dining table, plates of food in front of them.
Chandler, looking across the table, says, "Pass the cheese, please."
Joey, looking down at his plate, pushes a small bowl across the table toward Chandler without looking up.
Monica watches Joey, then turns her gaze toward Chandler. Joey continues to stare down at the table.
Monica looks closely at Joey and says, "My God, you can't even look at me, can you?"
Joey, still looking down at the table, replies, "No."
Monica continues to look at Joey, while Joey keeps his gaze lowered.
In an apartment kitchen, Chandler stands near a round dining table where Monica and Joey sit eating. Chandler smiles and gestures with one hand.
Chandler says, "Hey, Phoebe. We can talk to Phoebe." Phoebe walks past him wearing a blue dress.
Phoebe continues walking past Chandler, looking down.
Phoebe keeps walking and says, "No, I'm too depressed to talk." Chandler clasps his hands together and says, "I'll give you a talk to us." Phoebe exits the kitchen area.
Ross and Rachel walk into the living room area holding hands.
Ross, still holding Rachel's hand, asks, "Hey, you guys. What do you think about making that beach trip an annual thing?" Joey, Monica, and Chandler all simultaneously say, "No."
Rachel releases Ross's hand and steps forward.
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "All right, that's it. You guys, what happened out there?" Monica gestures with her fork and says, "What? We took a walk."
Monica shakes her head, continuing, "Nothing happened."
Monica stands up, holding a plate. Looking toward Rachel, she says, "I came back with nothing all over me," her volume slightly raised.
Rachel says, "Come on, what happened?" Ross looks toward Joey and says, "Joey."
Joey stands up from the table quickly.
Joey says, "All right." Monica steps toward him, pointing a finger, and says loudly, "No, Joey. We swore we'd never tell." Chandler also steps forward, pointing his finger at Joey.
Chandler keeps his finger pointed at Joey.
Chandler says, "They'll never understand."
Joey looks between Chandler and Monica, who both stare at him. He slightly lowers his head.
Joey gestures with both hands, raising his volume: "We have to say something. We have to get it out. It's eating me alive."
Monica exhales audibly, placing her hand near her forehead, and lowers her gaze.
Joey gestures toward Monica and says, "Monica got stung by a jellyfish." Chandler steps back slightly, raising his hands.
Chandler lowers his hands. Monica turns away slightly.
Chandler says, "All right," looking down.
Monica steps forward, looking downward, while Chandler moves slightly behind her.
Monica says softly, "I got stung," keeping her arms at her sides.
Monica briefly looks up, then down again.
Monica continues softly, "Stung bad."
Monica shakes her head slightly, her gaze still lowered.
Monica gestures with one hand and says, "I couldn't stand."
Monica briefly glances toward the others.
Monica continues, "I couldn't walk." Chandler looks up and adds, "We were 2 miles from the house."
Chandler stands with his hands in his pockets, looking forward.
Chandler says, "We were scared and alone," his voice flat.
Chandler looks toward Monica.
Chandler gently places a hand on Monica's shoulder and says, "We didn't think we could make it."
Monica looks at Chandler, who removes his hand. She turns back to face forward.
Monica says, "I was in too much pain," her facial expression tense.
Joey points to himself and says, "And I was tired from digging the huge hole."
Ross and Rachel sit on a coffee table, watching the group intently.
Chandler points toward Joey and says, "And then Joey remembered something."
Joey gestures with his hands, looking at Ross and Rachel.
Joey says, "I'd seen this thing on the Discovery Channel." Ross points a finger and interrupts, "Wait a minute."
Ross continues pointing, saying, "I saw that. On the Discovery Channel?"
Ross nods and says, "Yeah, about jellyfish and how if you-" trailing off as his eyes widen.
Ross's jaw drops slightly as he stares at Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross scrunches his face and says, "Ew." Rachel looks at Monica, her eyes widening, and says, "You peed on yourself."
Phoebe reacts with her facial features drawn together.
Phoebe says, "Ew."
Monica quickly steps forward, raising one hand.
Monica points at Rachel, her volume rising as she says, "You can't say that. You don't know."
Monica looks away, keeping her arms slightly raised.
Monica gestures with both hands and says rapidly, "I thought I was going to pass out from the pain anyway."
Monica lowers her hands, looks down, and adds, "I tried, but I couldn't bend that way."
Phoebe looks at Monica with her mouth slightly open. Audience laughter continues.
Monica says softly, "So-"
Monica glances toward Joey.
Joey looks forward and says, "Yeah."
Joey straightens his posture and nods slightly.
Joey points to his chest with his thumb and says, "That's right, I stepped up."
Joey looks at the others, pointing at himself again.
Joey points firmly toward Monica and says, "She's my friend and she needed help."
Joey lowers his hand and looks around.
Joey points outward to the group and says, "If I had to, I'd pee on any one of you."
Ross, sitting on the table, looks at Joey with his eyebrows raised. Audience laughter peaks.
The group remains silent. Joey stands with his hands on his hips.
Joey looks down and says softly, "Only I couldn't."
Joey keeps his hands on his hips, his gaze still lowered.
Joey shrugs slightly and says, "I got the stage fright."
Joey scrunches his face and looks at the others.
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "I wanted to help, but there was just too much pressure."
Joey points a thumb back toward Chandler and says, "So I turned to Chandler." Audience laughter swells.
Chandler is sitting on an armchair, covering his face with his hands. Phoebe turns her head sharply toward him.
Chandler stands up quickly, extending his arms, and shouts loudly, "Joey kept screaming at me, do it now."
Chandler continues shouting, "Do it. Do it. Do it now."
Chandler's face remains tense, his mouth slightly open, as the audio of his shout fades.
In an apartment living room, Joey and Chandler stand near the kitchen area. Chandler looks downward before raising his eyes.
Chandler says, "Sometimes, late at night, I can still hear the screaming." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey smiles and looks at Chandler. Monica, Ross, and Rachel are gathered near the living room seating area, with Monica standing with her arms crossed.
Joey gestures slightly with his hand and says, "That's because sometimes I just do it through my wall to freak you out." Audience laughter follows.
Monica remains standing with her arms crossed, looking slightly downward.
Rachel turns her head toward Monica and asks, "Maybe there's somebody that you guys could talk to now." Monica replies, "Who?" while spreading her arms outward.
Rachel shakes her head slightly.
Monica, her arms still spread, says, "It's not like they have a group for people like us."
Phoebe, seated on the couch, looks up and opens her purse.
Phoebe reaches into her purse and says, "Yeah, no, there's this guy, Owen. He's the best."
Phoebe continues searching through her purse.
Phoebe says, "Might have his card," as she pulls a small item from her purse.
The setting changes to a nighttime view of city buildings and traffic, then to the inside of a coffee shop. Phoebe sits on a stool, playing an acoustic guitar.
Phoebe sings, "And mom," while strumming the guitar.
A woman, Phoebe Sr., enters the coffee shop through the front door as Phoebe continues playing.
Phoebe sings, "those are the 66 colors of my bedroom."
Phoebe finishes the song. Phoebe Sr. stands near the doorway, looking toward her.
Phoebe speaks into the microphone, "Thank you. Thank you. Oh, and I invite all of you to count the colors in your bedroom." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe spots Phoebe Sr. standing by the door and points a finger at her.
Phoebe says, "Except for you. You go away," lowering her hand.
Phoebe unstraps her guitar and begins placing it into its open case on a nearby table. Phoebe Sr. slowly approaches her.
Phoebe Sr. stands near Phoebe and says, "I'll go in a second. I just want to tell you that" as Phoebe focuses on her guitar case.
Phoebe Sr. continues, "there hasn't been a day when I didn't regret giving you up." Phoebe grabs the handle of her guitar case.
Phoebe lifts her guitar case from the table.
Phoebe says, "Okay, bye," and walks quickly past Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe Sr. turns and follows Phoebe as she walks toward the back of the coffee shop.
Walking behind her, Phoebe Sr. says, "No, I'm not done. I just want you to know that the reason I never looked you up was because,"
Phoebe stops and turns slightly to face Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe Sr. gestures with her hands and says, "well, I was afraid that you would react just, well, like the way you're reacting right now,"
Phoebe Sr. touches Phoebe's arm and says, "and can't we just"
Phoebe Sr. continues, "start from here, please?" keeping her hand on Phoebe's arm.
Phoebe looks at Phoebe Sr. with her lips pressed together.
Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "No."
Phoebe turns away and walks toward the coffee counter. Phoebe Sr. remains standing, clasping her hands in front of her.
Phoebe Sr. softly says, "Okay."
Phoebe Sr. keeps her hands clasped.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Sorry," with her gaze lowered.
Phoebe Sr. raises her head slightly as Phoebe continues interacting with the counter in the background.
Phoebe Sr. steps forward slightly and says, "But just one last thing."
Phoebe Sr. looks toward Phoebe.
Phoebe Sr. says, "You came looking for family."
Phoebe turns her head to look back at Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe Sr. touches her chest and says, "I'm family."
Phoebe maintains her gaze on Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe Sr. says, "I'm it."
Phoebe turns her body fully around to face Phoebe Sr., leaning her back against the counter.
Phoebe Sr. folds her hands and says, "Now I'm done."
Phoebe looks at Phoebe Sr., her mouth opening slightly as she shifts her weight.
Phoebe looks downward briefly, then back up, and says, "But it's not like we're losing anything."
Phoebe Sr. nods her head slightly.
Phoebe Sr. replies, "Yeah, I guess you're right."
Phoebe shifts her gaze slightly.
Phoebe says, "It's not like we know each other or anything. Or that we have anything in common."
Phoebe Sr. looks to the side briefly and replies, "Well, I don't know."
Phoebe Sr. continues, "It's not like we don't have anything in common."
Phoebe looks at her steadily.
Phoebe Sr. shrugs slightly and says,
Phoebe's eyes widen.
Phoebe points to her chest and says, "I like pizza."
Phoebe Sr. smiles and raises her index finger.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Wait, I like the Beatles."
Phoebe raises her head slightly and says loudly, "Oh, my God. So do I."
Both women laugh.
Phoebe extends her hands forward and says, "Wait."
Phoebe quickly asks, "Puppies: cute or ugly?"
Phoebe Sr. quickly replies, "Oh, so cute," raising her hands.
Both women laugh again, smiling at each other.
Phoebe's smile fades slightly as she says, "But I'm mad at you." Phoebe Sr. replies, "I know," her own smile fading.
Phoebe Sr. looks steadily at Phoebe.
Phoebe Sr. says softly, "I'm mad at me, too."
Phoebe looks downward briefly, then looks back up at Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe asks, "Well, do you want to get something to eat? Because I'm kind of hungry."
Phoebe Sr. nods.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Hey, me, too."
Off-screen, Phoebe says, "Uncanny."
Phoebe Sr. continues smiling as the clip ends.
In a coffee shop, Phoebe Sr. and Phoebe stand facing each other near the exit.
"It looks like it's going to rain and I don't have my umbrella," Phoebe Sr. says.

Phoebe Sr. begins, "Me neither. I really hate that—" but Phoebe interrupts, turning away to pick up a guitar case. "I'd stop it now, you're just doing it to freak me out," Phoebe says. Audience laughter begins.
Audience laughter continues as Phoebe walks out the door, and Phoebe Sr. follows her outside.
The scene changes to a dimly lit bedroom where three candles burn in the foreground. Ross and Rachel lie in bed, covered by a white blanket. Ross is shirtless and embraces Rachel.
"I missed you," Rachel says softly.
"I missed you, too," Ross replies, kissing her forehead.
Rachel shifts her position, wrapping her arms tighter around herself. Ross rests his chin near her head, keeping his eyes open.
"So nervous about that letter," Rachel says softly.
Ross continues resting his head against hers, maintaining a neutral expression.
"But the way you owned up to everything," Rachel says, gazing upward.
"It just showed me how much you've grown, you know?" Rachel continues.
Ross visibly swallows, his expression remaining still.
"I suppose," Ross says softly.
Rachel shifts her head slightly against his chest.
"You have, Ross. You have. You should give yourself credit," Rachel says.
"I mean, my mom never thought this would work out," Rachel says, her eyes narrowing slightly.
Ross keeps his gaze steady forward.
"She was all, once a cheater, always a cheater," Rachel says.
Ross's eyes shift and his jaw tightens slightly as he opens his mouth.
"Oh, I just wish we hadn't lost those four months," Rachel says, her voice soft.
Ross maintains his steady gaze.
"But if time was what you needed just to gain a little perspective," Rachel continues, raising one hand to stroke Ross's neck.
Ross's eyes widen as he abruptly breaks the embrace and sits up.
"We were on a break," Ross shouts loudly, prompting audience laughter. The setting changes to an apartment living room where Monica sits at a table wearing yellow rubber gloves.
Chandler and Joey rush into the apartment through the front door.
"Coffee house?" Chandler asks, turning back to the door. Monica stands up instantly and says, "You bet," tossing her gloves onto the table as audience laughter sounds.
Chandler, Joey, and Monica hurry out the door.
In the bedroom, Ross stands wearing a green sweater and sweatpants, pulling his pants up. "And for the record, it took two people to break up this relationship," he says.
Rachel, wearing a grey robe, stands facing him and replies, "Yeah, you and that girl from the coffee place." Audience laughter is audible.
"Which yesterday you took full responsibility for," Rachel says, pointing at him.
Ross gestures widely and responds, "I didn't know what I was taking responsibility for, okay?"
"I didn't finish the whole letter," Ross says. "What?" Rachel asks, her face tightening.
"I fell asleep," Ross says. "You fell asleep?" Rachel asks, her volume rising as audience laughter is heard.
Ross clenches his fists at his sides and inhales sharply.
"It was 5:30 in the morning," Ross says, stepping forward and gesturing.
"And you had rambled on for 18 pages," Ross continues, waving his hands widely.
Ross bends down and picks up his shoes from the floor.
"Front and back," Ross says, holding his shoes up, followed by audience laughter.
Ross turns and walks out of the bedroom into the living room, with Rachel following him.
In the living room, Ross turns around to face Rachel. "And by the way, Y-O-U apostrophe re means you are," he says, pointing at her, while Chandler, Joey, and Monica stand in the background.
"Y-O-U-R means your," Ross finishes, emphasizing the spelling. Audience laughter sounds.
Rachel throws her hands up in the air.
"I can't believe I even thought of getting back together with you. We are so over," Rachel yells, stepping toward him.
Ross stammers briefly, pointing his finger back at her.
"Fine by—" Ross shouts, turning toward the front door as the audience laughs.
Rachel walks quickly toward the bedroom, then spins around to face Ross again.
"Those little spelling tips will come in handy when you're at home on Saturday nights playing Scrabble with Monica," Rachel yells.
Monica, standing in the background, says, "Hey," prompting audience laughter.
"Sorry," Rachel says, glancing toward Monica.
Rachel turns her attention back to Ross, placing her hands on her hips.
"I just feel bad about all that sleep you're gonna miss wishing you were with—" Rachel says.
Ross interrupts, stepping toward her, "No, don't you worry about me falling asleep."
"I still have your letter," Ross says, tapping his chest. Audience laughter sounds.
Ross turns around and opens the front door.
Rachel steps forward and shouts, "And, hey, just so you know, it's not that common."
"It doesn't happen to every guy, and it is a big deal," Rachel yells as Ross walks into the hallway and shuts the door behind him. Audience laughter begins.
Audience laughter continues as Chandler steps quickly forward from the kitchen area, pointing at the door.
"I knew it," Chandler says, followed by more audience laughter.
The screen turns black.
The scene changes to Monica and Chandler sitting across from each other at the kitchen table holding playing cards.
"Gin," Monica says, laying her cards face up on the table.
Chandler looks down at his cards.
"We were playing gin," Chandler says, looking up.
Chandler continues examining his cards, then looks back up at Monica.
"You know, if we were a couple, we could play this game naked," Chandler says.
Monica smiles and laughs slightly while looking at him, and audience laughter sounds.
"When you stop," Monica says, smiling. Chandler replies, "Okay."
"All right," Chandler says, putting his cards down and picking up a small bottle.
Chandler takes a sip from the bottle.
"Okay. All right," Chandler says, setting the bottle down.
"I think you're great," Monica says, resting her hands together on the table.
Chandler looks at her, listening.
"I think you're sweet and you're smart and I love you," Monica continues.
Chandler smiles slightly.
"But you will always be the guy who peed on me," Monica says, prompting loud audience laughter.
Chandler's smile fades and his expression turns still. The screen turns black.
On a black screen, white text reads "CREATED BY DAVID CRANE & MARTA KAUFFMAN." Instrumental music plays.

The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Chandler stands facing Monica, who holds a glass. Joey stands next to Chandler, and Rachel is visible in the background behind Monica. Audience cheering is audible. Chandler raises his right hand, pointing his index finger upward, and says, "So... we're gonna take a walk."
"Yeah," Joey says. He and Chandler turn and walk toward the doorway. Rachel closes her eyes and rubs her forehead with one hand, while Monica brings the glass to her lips.
